canonical_truth.summary: Pale seedling shoots emerge from waterlogged soil at the flood-disturbed ground near the west forage site — thin stems, translucent, clustered low where drainage slowed.
canonical_truth.location: west forage site and flood-adjacent ground
canonical_truth.concrete_outcome: YY encounters the first visible growth returning to the flooded site — nothing harvestable, but the ground is visibly beginning to recover.

perception_prompt.noticeable_surface: pale seedling shoots pushing through wet mud where the ground was disturbed by the flood — thin stems, translucent, clustered low

authorial_note.real_world_inspiration: Earth Day 2026 (April 22) — global day of environmental renewal; planting and seedling campaigns in 190+ countries worldwide
authorial_note.translation_logic: The flooding from day 8 churned and soaked the soil, creating prime germination conditions; YY's encounter with recovery is physical — stems through mud, not concepts
authorial_note.restraint_reason: The event enters through what YY sees at the flooded site; Earth Day itself is never mentioned — the seedlings are the landing
